28.$description: Input surface alpha — black at 28%; countdown border alpha — blue-vivid at 28%

default.$description: Opacity for Color/Overlay/Default (Black × 78%). Figma: bind fill opacity on Color/Overlay/Default paint. CSS: use rgba(0,0,0,0.78) directly — the .modal-backdrop is a separate element with no text children, so either approach is safe.

glass.$description: Opacity component for Color/Surface/Glass (Neutral 800 × 74%). Figma: bind fill opacity on Color/Surface/Glass paint. CSS: use rgba(28,28,30,0.74) directly — do not use element-level opacity on cards or modals.

input.$description: Opacity component for Color/Surface/Input (Black × 28%). Figma: bind fill opacity on Color/Surface/Input paint. CSS: use rgba(0,0,0,0.28) directly.

glass.$description: Opacity component for Color/Border/Glass (White × 8%). Figma: bind stroke opacity. CSS: use rgba(255,255,255,0.08) directly.

control.$description: Opacity component for Color/Border/Control (White × 10%). Figma: bind stroke opacity. CSS: use rgba(255,255,255,0.10) directly.

surface.$description: Opacity component for Color/Danger/Surface (Red 500 × 20%). Figma: bind fill opacity. CSS: use rgba(168,75,75,0.20) directly.

border.$description: Opacity component for Color/Countdown/Border (Blue Vivid × 28%). Figma: bind stroke opacity. CSS: use rgba(10,132,255,0.28) directly.

default.$description: Opacity for disabled component state. Applied as element-level opacity (opacity: 0.4) on the full component, not on individual fills, because disabled state dims all visual properties uniformly.

overlay.$description: Opacity for trip header background image dim overlay. Applied via ::before/::after pseudo-element, not on the container, so child elements remain fully opaque.

hover.$description: Opacity for interactive hover overlay fills. Applied as an additional paint layer on the component fill stack, not as element-level opacity.
